UniGetUI is an application that makes ma: UniGetUI is an application that makes managing your software easier, by providing an all-in-one graphical interface for your command-line package managers.
Report an issue or submit a feature requ: Report an issue or submit a feature request
Using UniGetUI implies the acceptation o: Using UniGetUI implies the acceptance of the MIT License
Change how operations request administra: Change how operations request administrator rights
Ask for administrator privileges once fo: Ask for administrator privileges once for each batch of operations
Ask only once for administrator privileg: Ask only once for administrator privileges
Prohibit any kind of Elevation via UniGe: Prohibit any kind of Elevation via UniGetUI Elevator or GSudo
This option WILL cause issues. Any opera: This option WILL cause issues. Any operation incapable of elevating itself WILL FAIL. Install/update/uninstall as administrator will NOT WORK.
Custom command-line arguments can change: Custom command-line arguments can change the way in which programs are installed, upgraded or uninstalled, in a way UniGetUI cannot control. Using custom command-lines can break packages. Proceed with caution.
Ignore custom pre-install and post-insta: Ignore custom pre-install and post-install commands when importing packages from a bundle
Pre and post install commands will be ru: Pre and post install commands will be run before and after a package gets installed, upgraded or uninstalled. Be aware that they may break things unless used carefully
Allow changing the paths for package man: Allow changing the paths for package manager executables
Turning this on enables changing the exe: Turning this on enables changing the executable file used to interact with package managers. While this allows finer-grained customization of your install processes, it may also be dangerous
Allow importing custom command-line argu: Allow importing custom command-line arguments when importing packages from a bundle
Malformed command-line arguments can bre: Malformed command-line arguments can break packages, or even allow a malicious actor to gain privileged execution. Therefore, importing custom command-line arguments is disabled by default.
Allow importing custom pre-install and p: Allow importing custom pre-install and post-install commands when importing packages from a bundle
Pre and post install commands can do ver: Pre and post install commands can do very nasty things to your device, if designed to do so. It can be very dangerous to import the commands from a bundle, unless you trust the source of that package bundle
Administrator rights and other dangerous: Administrator rights and other dangerous settings

There are two main reasons to not run Wi:
There are two main reasons to not run UniGetUI as administrator:
The first one is that the Scoop package manager might cause problems with some commands when ran with administrator rights.
The second one is that running UniGetUI as administrator means that any package that you download will be ran as administrator (and this is not safe).
Remember that if you need to install a specific package as administrator, you can always right-click the item -> Install/Update/Uninstall as administrator.
There is an installation in progress. If: There is an installation in progress. If you close UniGetUI, the installation may fail and have unexpected results. Do you still want to quit UniGetUI?

While Winget can be used within WingetUI: While WinGet can be used within UniGetUI, UniGetUI can be used with other package managers, which can be confusing. In the past, UniGetUI was designed to work only with Winget, but this is not true anymore, and therefore UniGetUI does not represent what this project aims to become.

WingetUI autostart behaviour, applicatio: UniGetUI autostart behaviour, application launch settings
WingetUI can check if your software has: UniGetUI can check if your software has available updates, and install them automatically if you want
WingetUI has not been machine translated: UniGetUI has not been machine translated! The following users have been in charge of the translations:
WingetUI is being renamed in order to em: UniGetUI is being renamed in order to emphasize the difference between UniGetUI (the interface you are using right now) and WinGet (a package manager developed by Microsoft with which I am not related)
WingetUI is being updated. When finished: UniGetUI is being updated. When finished, UniGetUI will restart itself
WingetUI is free, and it will be free fo: UniGetUI is free, and it will be free forever. No ads, no credit card, no premium version. 100% free, forever.
WingetUI uses the following libraries. W: UniGetUI uses the following libraries. Without them, UniGetUI wouldn't have been possible.
WingetUI will not check for updates peri: UniGetUI will not check for updates periodically. They will still be checked at launch, but you won't be warned about them.
WingetUI will show a UAC prompt every ti: UniGetUI will show a UAC prompt every time a package requires elevation to be installed.
WingetUI will soon be named {newname}. T: WingetUI will soon be named {newname}. This will not represent any change in the application. I (the developer) will continue the development of this project as I am doing right now, but under a different name.
WingetUI wouldn't have been possible wit: UniGetUI wouldn't have been possible without the help of our dear contributors. Check out their GitHub profiles, UniGetUI wouldn't be possible without them!
